reason: Pulp Fiction is famously told out of chronological order, weaving interlinked vignettes into a nonlinear puzzle.
The Usual Suspects unfolds through unreliable flashbacks and a twist that reshapes the entire narrative.
Reservoir Dogs uses a fractured timeline to reveal motivations and piece together the real heist story.
Lost Highway’s reality-bending structure loops and fractures time, making you question what’s real.
The Game toys with reality and chronology as a staged conspiracy that you must decode alongside the protagonist.
Sliding Doors shows parallel timelines split by a single moment, inviting you to compare and piece both arcs.
Citizen Kane uses disordered flashbacks from multiple perspectives to assemble the true story of Charles Kane.
Twelve Monkeys jumps through time in a mind-bending loop, scattering clues that you must assemble into its mystery.
Strange Days splices found memories and fractured recollections into a dark puzzle of perception and reality.
Groundhog Day loops the same day repeatedly, letting you piece together subtle changes in chronology.
